slides.head:
$120 million.
One vote today.
Members own the bill.
slides.body: a co-op decides behind a closed door

slides.head:
A turbine, and a
$120 million question
slides.body: On July 13, in a closed executive session, Golden Valley Electric Association's board weighs buying a second LM6000 gas turbine for its North Pole plant. The price is about $120 million. In April the board spent $50,000 just to hold a place in the order line.
The News-Miner reports demand for these turbines has surged because large data centers need a guaranteed power supply.
slides.body2: GVEA points to general growth. "We did see higher load across the board," President Travis Million said, running diesel units harder because the co-op is "not getting any energy from Southcentral."
labels: The co-op has not said the turbine is for data centers.

slides.head:
One machine.
Steel and megawatts.
slides.body: An LM6000 makes 45 to 58 megawatts on its own, or 76 to 152 paired with steam or storage. The plant's first LM6000, from 2006, still runs at 47 megawatts. Ordering another can take three to five years.

slides.head:
Members just absorbed
the biggest fuel hike on record.
slides.body: On June 1 the fuel charge jumped 61 percent, the largest increase GVEA has ever posted. The fuel its plants burn more than doubled since February.

slides.head:
The loads are still
on paper.
slides.body: The Air Force is offering about 4,700 acres, 12 parcels across three bases, to companies that would build AI data centers. Two of them, Eielson and Clear, are in the Interior. None is built, no lease is signed. A pending defense bill would require a grid study, if it becomes law.
labels: JBER is in Anchorage, not on the Interior grid.

slides.head: Firm power, soft load.
soft: grid study  would require, if enacted

slides.head:
The bill is certain.
The load is a maybe.
slides.body: Members are being asked to finance firm, dated capacity now, while the loads that justify it stay on paper. And the vote happens in executive session, a room members reach only through board elections.
labels: More gas and diesel also burns in a borough the EPA rates "Serious" for fine-particle pollution.

slides.head: Should a co-op spend $120 million on firm power while the loads that need it are still proposals? Or wait until someone signs for the load?

caption:
Golden Valley Electric's board weighs $120 million in new gas capacity today, and its members carry the debt.

The turbine would be firm, dated, gas-fired power. The AI data-center loads that make firm capacity feel urgent are still acreage and proposals on Air Force land. Meanwhile GVEA members just absorbed the largest fuel-charge increase in the co-op's history.

Nine slides on what members already paid, what the machine actually buys, and which loads are still on paper. The vote happens in executive session.

Should members finance firm capacity now, or wait until a load actually signs?

#AlaskaEnergy #GVEA #AIDataCenters
